input.title: CRM System
input.description: CRM System is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Python Flask, PostgreSQL for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Contact Management
requirements: Add contacts with name, email, phone, company

FR-02.title: Deal Pipeline
requirements: Create deals with value, stage, and close date
Multiple pipelines for different sales processes

FR-03.title: Activity Tracking
requirements: Activity timeline per contact and deal

FR-05.title: Task Management
requirements: Create tasks with due date and assignee

FR-07.title: Lead Scoring
requirements: Score leads based on configurable criteria
Score decay over time for inactivity

FR-08.title: Workflow Automation
requirements: Create automated workflows with triggers and actions
Triggers: deal stage change, new contact, score threshold
Actions: send email, create task, update field, notify

FR-09.title: Company Management
requirements: Create company records with industry and size

FR-10.title: Dashboard and Analytics
requirements: Key metrics: total deals, revenue, conversion rate
Export data to CSV and PDF

FR-01.title: Design Contact Management UI
FR-01.description: Design the user interface for contact management. Create the screens, forms, dashboards, or status views needed by the SRS and make the workflow clear, responsive, and accessible for the target users.
acceptance_criteria: UI design for contact management is complete and reviewable.
The contact management design is responsive and accessible.
The design for contact management aligns with the documented user flow and validation states.

FR-01.title: Frontend Implementation of Contact Management
FR-01.description: Implement the frontend behavior for contact management. Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: Add contacts with name, email, phone, company; Import contacts from CSV; Tag and categorize contacts.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend implements the required contact management user interactions.
Validation, loading, and error states for contact management are handled in the UI.
Frontend behavior for contact management is integrated correctly with backend APIs.

FR-01.title: Backend Implementation of Contact Management
FR-01.description: Implement the backend logic for contact management. Build the required APIs, business rules, validation, permissions, persistence, and processing needed to support these behaviors: Add contacts with name, email, phone, company; Import contacts from CSV; Tag and categorize contacts.
acceptance_criteria: Backend logic for contact management is implemented and testable.
APIs and business rules for contact management enforce validation and security requirements.
Data handling for contact management is reliable and supports the required workflow.

FR-01.title: Integrate Contact Management with External Services
FR-01.description: Integrate contact management with the external services or providers referenced by the SRS. Implement secure service connections, payload validation, error handling, and recovery behavior for any third-party dependency involved in the workflow.
acceptance_criteria: External or cross-system integrations required by contact management exchange the expected payloads successfully.
Retry, timeout, and failure handling for contact management integrations are implemented and observable.
Credentials, tokens, and outbound calls used by contact management are configured and secured correctly.

FR-01.title: Testing Contact Management Functionality
FR-01.description: Create and execute tests for contact management. Cover the main success flows, failure scenarios, permission checks, validation rules, integrations, and operational edge cases described in the SRS so the implementation is safe to release.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the main success and failure scenarios for contact management.
Security, validation, and permission checks for contact management are verified.
Regression coverage exists for the most important contact management behaviors.
